> **Note:** No US trading session on 2026-06-03. Figures are from the last session (2026-06-02).

## Market Overview

U.S. equities advanced modestly on June 3, with the S&P 500 extending its winning streak to nine consecutive sessions despite what analysts are calling a "breadth paradox." The benchmark index rose 0.13%, while the Nasdaq 100 gained 0.48% and the Dow added 0.45%. Notably, small caps outperformed with the Russell 2000 climbing 0.90%, suggesting some broadening of the rally beyond mega-cap tech.

## AI Infrastructure Rally Dominates

The session was defined by dramatic divergence within the technology sector, particularly along the AI infrastructure stack. Networking and interconnect companies surged 11.83%, while semiconductors and compute rose 5.34%. **Marvell Technology** led top gainers with a stunning 32.52% surge after Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang reportedly called it the "next trillion-dollar company." Other AI beneficiaries followed suit: **Hewlett Packard Enterprise** jumped 19.47% on a massive earnings beat, while **Aehr Test Systems** gained 20.70%.

Among megacap chips, the picture was mixed. Broadcom rallied 4.70%, Micron advanced 2.76%, and AMD gained 2.24%, while Apple climbed 2.90% ahead of its WWDC event. However, Microsoft tumbled 4.17% and Alphabet fell 3.81%—the latter coinciding with news of an $80 billion equity raise to fund AI infrastructure. Nvidia dipped 0.69% despite positive broker commentary, while hyperscalers and SaaS stocks broadly underperformed.

**Legend Biotech** topped all gainers with a 42.22% spike, though healthcare as a sector remained subdued.

## Energy and Cross-Asset Signals

Crude oil climbed 2.71% to $96.30, approaching the $100 threshold despite geopolitical uncertainty in the Middle East. This move comes as the Strategic Petroleum Reserve remains near 40-year lows. The 10-year Treasury yield eased 2 basis points to 4.45%, while the VIX ticked up 1.78% to 16.05—a modest rise that suggests underlying caution despite the surface-level calm.

Gold declined 0.47%, while Bitcoin slipped marginally. The dollar edged higher against the yen and held steady against the euro, reflecting stable rate expectations.

## Themes and Outlook

The action underscores a rotation within tech: hardware and infrastructure providers are capturing investor attention while software and hyperscalers face profit-taking. News of Alphabet's capital raise and Berkshire Hathaway's $10 billion investment in Google highlights the scale of AI infrastructure spending. Meanwhile, Reddit chatter warned of an impending "rugpull," reflecting retail skepticism even as institutional money pours into AI plays. Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon's comment that markets are in "greed mode" captures the sentiment—momentum is strong, but selectivity is rising.

> **Note:** No US trading session on 2026-06-04. Figures are from the last session (2026-06-03).

## Market Overview

U.S. equities retreated Thursday, snapping a nine-day winning streak for the S&P 500, with cyclicals and mega-cap tech bearing the brunt of selling pressure. The S&P 500 fell 0.74%, while the Dow Jones posted a sharper 1.21% decline and the Russell 2000 dropped 1.31%. The Nasdaq 100 showed relative resilience, down just 0.29%, buoyed by strength in memory chip stocks. The VIX edged up 3.36% to 16.60, reflecting modest risk-off sentiment.

## Sector Rotation and AI Stack Divergence

A pronounced divergence emerged within the AI technology stack. Memory stocks surged 3.51%, with **SanDisk** (+6.71%), **Intel** (+4.43%), **AMD** (+4.02%), **Meta** (+4.24%), and Micron (+1.45%) leading the charge. This strength contrasts sharply with weakness in hyperscalers, which fell 3.17% as Microsoft (-3.17%), Nvidia (-3.62%), and Amazon (-2.53%) all declined. SaaS names underperformed badly, down 6.99%, suggesting investors are rotating within tech rather than abandoning the AI trade entirely.

Broadcom's post-earnings slide in after-hours trading—driven by disappointing software revenue and AI chip forecasts—underscores concerns about near-term demand visibility. CrowdStrike also fell despite beating estimates, highlighting stretched valuations in select cybersecurity names.

## Top Gainers and Standout Movers

Among broader market gainers, **Abivax SA (ABVX)** surged 24.34%, while **Navitas Semiconductor (NVTS)** jumped 19.26% and **MiniMed Group (MMED)** climbed 15.94%. **POET Technologies** added 11.29%, reflecting enthusiasm for emerging semiconductor and photonics plays riding the broader AI infrastructure wave. Lightwave Logic also reportedly soared 15% on Nvidia-related optimism.

## Cross-Asset Signals

Safe-haven flows dominated fixed income and commodities. Gold rallied 1.36% to $4,497, while crude oil slipped 1.08%. The 10-year Treasury yield rose 4 basis points to 4.49%, suggesting mixed inflation or growth concerns rather than pure flight-to-quality. Bitcoin dropped 2.31% to $62,538, triggering billions in liquidations as crypto investors reportedly "rage quit" amid AI equities outperformance.

## Key Developments

News flow centered on IPO activity and Fed leadership changes. SpaceX filed for a record $75 billion IPO at $135 per share (though Morningstar values it at less than half the $1.77 trillion target), while quantum computing firm Quantinuum raised $1.68 billion. Fed Chair Warsh's first hires, including a "Project 2025" author, drew attention but yielded no immediate policy signals. Anthropic's AI scaling announcement and Morgan Stanley's move to open wealth management to AI agents further underscored the AI infrastructure theme dominating investor attention.
